frameworks.name: working memory population coding
frameworks.one_line: neural population representations of multiple remembered items in PFC

frameworks.name: feature binding / misbinding
frameworks.one_line: swap errors as breakdown of object-feature binding across items

frameworks.name: selective readout from working memory
frameworks.one_line: error emerges when target is selected, not during storage

core_question: What neural mechanism gives rise to swap errors when multiple items are held in working memory?
key_assumptions: swap errors reflect misattribution of features between memoranda
neural population activity during delay encodes multiple items jointly
behavioral swap errors are a tractable correlate of binding failure
manipulations: retrospective vs prospective cueing of which item to report
multiple colored items at different locations
comparison of correct trials vs swap-error trials
key_findings: monkeys made swap errors in both retrospective and prospective tasks
neural correlates of swap errors emerged at the selection-from-memory stage
on swap trials the distractor color was represented as if it were the target
interpretations: swap errors arise from a misdirected selection/readout, not faulty storage
binding failures can be localized to the moment information is selected from working memory
